titlestmt.titleproper: Finding Aid for the Nan Burrows Dominatrix Collection LSC.2410
titlestmt.author: Finding aid prepared by Kelly Besser, 2025.

p.date: Online finding aid last updated 2026 March 11 by Caroline Cubé.

address.addressline: Room A1713, Charles E. Young Research Library

publicationstmt.p: Finding aid description and metadata in this record are licensed under a Creative Commons Public Domain Designation (CC0 1.0 Universal). See LSC's Metadata Rights Statement for more information.

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-03-11 12:10:10 -0700.

did.abstract: Nan Burrows, born in 1939, is a Los Angeles based dominatrix who counted celebrities, heads of government, and corporations among her clients, was the master of "slaves" and led a parallel life as a devoted wife and mother. The Nan Burrows dominatrix collection ranges from 1933-1999 and is comprised of hundreds of letters and replies, numerous personal photographs, rare magazines, advertisements, flyers, zines, and artifacts of the BDSM subculture in Southern California.
did.physloc: Stored off-site. Advance notice is required for access to the collection. All requests to access special collections material must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Open for research. All requests to access special collections materials must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Property rights to the objects belong to UCLA Library Special Collections. All other rights, including copyright, are retained by the creators and their heirs. It is the responsibility of the researcher to determine who holds the copyright and pursue the copyright owner or his or her heir for permission to publish where The UC Regents do not hold the copyright.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Johnson Rare Books & Archives with funds from LACC, 2021.

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: First purchased by Johnson Rare Books & Archives from a vendor and then from the family.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: [Identification of item], Nan Burrows dominatrix collection (Collection 2410). UCLA Library Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, University of California, Los Angeles.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived user interest and research value, availability of staff and resources, and competing priorities. Library Special Collections provides a standard level of preservation and access for all collections and, when time and resources permit, conducts more intensive processing. These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards and best practices.

We are committed to providing ethical, inclusive, and anti-racist description of the materials we steward, and to remediating existing description of our materials that contains language that may be offensive or cause harm. We invite you to submit feedback about how our collections are described, and how they could be described more accurately, by filling out the form located on our website: Report Problematic Content and Description in UCLA's library collections and archives.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Nan Burrows, born in 1939, is a Los Angeles based dominatrix who counted celebrities, heads of government, and corporations among her clients, was the master of "slaves" and led a parallel life as a devoted wife and mother. Burrows — true to form — dominated the BDSM subculture in Southern California from the late 1950s through the 1980s. Now a widower and a grandmother, Burrows is living a quiet life in a suburban Southern California community.
Source: Johnson Rare Books and Archives. Dealer description, 2021.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: The Nan Burrows dominatrix collection ranges from 1933-1999 and is comprised of hundreds of letters and replies, numerous personal photographs, rare magazines, advertisements, flyers, zines, and artifacts of the BDSM subculture in Southern California. The materials were created for use in participation by Nan Burrows in the bondage community. Correspondence, advertisements, and publications were all used by Burrows to promote her services and interact with her communities.
Source: Johnson Rare Books and Archives. Dealer description, 2021.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This collection is arranged according to its existing order.
